body:
## Feature Request: Display session timer and per-message elapsed time in TUI

## Description

Show timing information in the TUI to help users monitor session duration and per-message response time.

### Session Timer

Show total elapsed time since TUI started, placed in a corner of the status bar:

```
‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
‚îÇ Turn 12/200 ‚îÇ mimo:pro ‚îÇ thinking...  ‚îÇ 03:42  ‚îÇ
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
                                       ^^^^^^ session timer
```

Format: `MM:SS` when under 1 hour, `HH:MM:SS` after.

### Per-Message Elapsed Time

When the agent finishes processing a user message and displays its response, show how long the entire round took (from user sending the message to agent finishing its response, including all intermediate tool calls and LLM turns):

```
> user: Â∏ÆÊàëÈáçÊûÑËøô‰∏™Ê®°Âùó
> agent: Â•ΩÁöÑÔºåÊàëÊù•Áúã‰∏Ä‰∏ã... (8.3s)
```

This is the **wall-clock time for the full user message round-trip**, not a single LLM API call. It covers all turns the agent internally makes to process that one user message.

### Use Cases

1. **Session timer**: Quickly know how long the current session has been running, useful for long tasks
2. **Per-message timing**: 
   -ÊÑüÁü•Ê®°ÂûãÂìçÂ∫îÈÄüÂ∫¶ÔºåÂèëÁé∞ÂèòÊÖ¢ÊàñË¢´ÈôêÈÄü
   - ÂØπÊØî‰∏çÂêåÊ®°Âûã/‰ªªÂä°ÁöÑÂ§ÑÁêÜÊïàÁéá
   - ÂøÉÁêÜÈ¢ÑÊúüÁÆ°ÁêÜÔºåÁü•ÈÅìÂ§ßÊ¶ÇË¶ÅÁ≠âÂ§ö‰πÖ

## Environment

- OS: Windows 10 x64
- koda-agent: latest
title: Feature Request: Display session timer and per-message elapsed time in TUI

body:
## Feature Request: Display max_turns in TUI status bar

## Description

Currently the TUI mode doesn't show the configured `max_turns` limit anywhere. Users have no way to know how many turns are remaining before the agent hits the hard stop.

## Suggested Behavior

Show the turn counter in the TUI status bar, for example:

```
Turn 12/200 | mimo:pro | thinking...
```

This would:
1. Let users see the configured max_turns at a glance
2. Show progress (current turn / max turns)
3. Help users decide whether to increase the limit for complex tasks

## Related

- `KODA_MAX_TURNS` env var and `llms.toml` `max_turns` field are already supported by the runtime (see #5)
- `goal` mode already has `--max-turns` flag with a known default of 50
- The turn loop is `for turn in 1..=self.cfg.max_turns` in `koda-agent-core`

## Environment

- OS: Windows 10 x64
- koda-agent: latest
title: Feature Request: Display current turn / max_turns in TUI status bar

body:
## Feature Request: Add `--max-turns` option to one-shot and TUI modes

## Background

`koda-agent goal` already supports `--max-turns` to control the maximum agent turns:

```bash
koda-agent goal "objective" --max-turns 200
```

However, the one-shot mode (`--input`) and TUI mode (`tui`) do not expose this option. The turn limit appears to be hardcoded (70 turns based on observed behavior).

## Observed Behavior

```bash
# goal mode: ‚úÖ supports --max-turns
koda-agent goal "task" --max-turns 200

# one-shot mode: ‚ùå no --max-turns, hardcoded to 70
koda-agent --profile glm --model default --input "hello"
# Runs until 70 turns, then stops

# TUI mode: ‚ùå no way to configure max turns
koda-agent tui
```

## Requested Behavior

1. Add `--max-turns <N>` to the global options (one-shot mode) so it works with `--input` and `--llm`
2. Add a `[defaults]` field `max_turns` in `llms.toml` for persistent configuration
3. Optionally, add a TUI slash command like `/max-turns 200` for runtime adjustment

## Suggested Config

```toml
[defaults]
stream = true
timeout_secs = 1200
max_tokens = 16384
max_turns = 200        # ‚Üê new field
failover = true
```

## Environment

- OS: Windows 10 x64
- koda-agent: latest

comments.body:
## Update: Source Code Analysis

Checked the source code in `crates/koda-agent-core/src/lib.rs`. The runtime already fully supports `max_turns`:

### Current Support

1. **Environment variable**: `KODA_MAX_TURNS` (highest priority)
2. **TOML config**: `max_turns` or `max_turn` field in profile/defaults
3. **Hardcoded default**: 70 (`.unwrap_or(70)`)
4. **Turn loop**: `for turn in 1..=self.cfg.max_turns`

### Bug: `config set` doesn't expose `max_turns`

The runtime reads `max_turns` from TOML, but the CLI `config set` command rejects it:

```
koda-agent config set glm max_turns 200
# Error: unsupported profile field `max_turns`
```

The field is silently ignored by `config set` but would work if manually edited into `llms.toml`.

### Workaround

Setting the env var works immediately:

- Windows (permanent): `[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('KODA_MAX_TURNS', '200', 'User')`
- Linux/Mac: `export KODA_MAX_TURNS=200`

### Suggested Fix

1. Add `max_turns` to the allowed fields in `config set` for both profiles and defaults
2. Optionally add `--max-turns` to `--input` one-shot mode (as originally requested)
3. Update `configuration.zh.md` docs to document this field

title: Feature Request: Add --max-turns option to one-shot and TUI modes

body:
## Bug Description

When using `kind = "native_claude"` with a non-Anthropic provider (e.g. ZhipuAI's Anthropic-compatible endpoint), the agent enters an infinite loop of "No-tool response requires another turn" until the turn limit is exhausted (70 turns).

The API connection and authentication succeed ‚Äî the model does respond ‚Äî but the agent's `native_claude` response parser fails to recognize the response content, treating every response as needing another turn.

## Reproduction

```bash
# Add a profile using native_claude kind with a third-party Anthropic-compatible endpoint
koda-agent config add glm \
  --kind native_claude \
  --base-url "https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/anthropic" \
  --api-key-env ZHIPUAI_API_KEY \
  --model "glm-5.1"

# Set a valid API key
koda-agent config secret ZHIPUAI_API_KEY

# Temporarily disable other profiles to pass global validation
# Then run:
koda-agent --profile glm --model default --input "hello, introduce yourself in one sentence"
```

## Observed Behavior

```
**LLM Running (Turn 1) ...**
[Info] No-tool response requires another turn.
**LLM Running (Turn 2) ...**
[Info] No-tool response requires another turn.
... (repeats 70 times until turn limit)
```

Key observations:
- **No network errors**: The API connection succeeds, no 401/404/timeout
- **Model responds**: The LLM call completes each turn without error
- **Parser fails**: The agent treats every response as empty/invalid and requests another turn
- **No output**: The model's actual response content is never displayed or returned

## Possible Causes

1. **Response format mismatch**: ZhipuAI's Anthropic-compatible endpoint may return slightly different `content_block` structure, `stop_reason` value, or missing fields compared to native Anthropic API
2. **stop_reason differences**: Anthropic uses `end_turn` / `tool_use` / `max_tokens`, but ZhipuAI might return different values (e.g. `stop` instead of `end_turn`)
3. **Content block structure**: The response might use a different content block type or wrapper format that the parser doesn't handle

## Suggested Fixes

1. **Add verbose/debug logging**: When `--verbose` is enabled, log the raw LLM response so users can inspect the actual format and identify the mismatch
2. **Tolerant parsing**: Make the `native_claude` parser more tolerant of response format variations from compatible providers
3. **Document supported providers**: Clearly state which providers are tested with `native_claude` kind, and note that third-party Anthropic-compatible endpoints may not work

## Environment

- OS: Windows 10 x64
- koda-agent: latest
- Profile: glm (native_claude kind)
- Endpoint: ZhipuAI Anthropic-compatible (`https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/anthropic`)
- Model: glm-5.1
title: native_claude kind causes infinite loop with third-party Anthropic-compatible providers
